title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R package containing Bayesian hierarchical Integral Projection Model (IPM) studying tree population dynamics (population growth rates, coexistence, sensitivity) eastern North America. Written R C++ acceleration via RcppEigen.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: External data path stored _data.path (plain text file pointing data directory local machine).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: vital_rates.R: Growth (von Bertalanffy), survival (logistic), ingrowth/recruitment functions parameterized function size, competition (basal area), climate. demographic models fitted inventory data. kernel.R: Assembles IPM kernels (P matrix = growth × survival, F matrix = recruitment) using numerical integration size classes BasalArea_competition.R: Computes competition metrics (intra/interspecific basal area) plot data matrix_image.R: Visualize IPM kernel discretized matrix src/eigen.cpp: C++ eigenvalue solver (RcppEigen), compiled via Rcpp load

title: Authors and Citation
text: Willian Vieira. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Vieira W (2026). forestIPM: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model Tree Population Dynamics. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/willvieira/forestIPM.
code: @Manual{,   title = {forestIPM: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics},   author = {Willian Vieira},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/willvieira/forestIPM}, }

what: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: Bayesian hierarchical Integral Projection Model (IPM) studying tree population dynamics eastern North America. package implements growth, survival, recruitment vital rate models parameterized forest inventory data, enabling researchers compute population growth rates, project stand dynamics, analyse demographic sensitivities 31 tree species.

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: Full documentation, methodology, worked examples available companion book: Forest Demography IPM Book function reference available package website.

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("willvieira/forestIPM")

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: use package research, please cite companion book.

title: Configure IPM projection settings — control
text: Configure IPM projection settings

title: Configure IPM projection settings — control
code: control(   years = 100,   delta_time = 1,   store_every = 1,   bin_width = 1,   compute_lambda = FALSE,   progress = TRUE )

title: Configure IPM projection settings — control
text: years Positive integer. Number simulation timesteps. Default 100. delta_time Positive numeric. Duration timestep years. Default 1. store_every Positive integer. Store stand state every N timesteps. Default 1. bin_width Positive integer. Bin width IPM kernel discretization. Default 1. compute_lambda Logical. Whether compute asymptotic lambda timestep via eigendecomposition. Set FALSE skip (faster projections population structure needed). Default FALSE progress Logical. Whether display progress bar projection. Default TRUE.

title: Configure IPM projection settings — control
text: object S3 class "ipm_control".

title: Configure IPM projection settings — control
code: ctrl <- control(years = 10, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) print(ctrl) #> <ipm_control>  10 years | dt=1.0 | store_every=1 | bin_width=1 | lambda=yes | progress=no

what: Convert Individual Tree Sizes to a Mesh-Aligned Size Distribution — dbh_to_sizeDist
title: Convert Individual Tree Sizes to a Mesh-Aligned Size Distribution — dbh_to_sizeDist
text: Bins individual tree diameter observations mesh point grid defined init_pop() output object.

title: Convert Individual Tree Sizes to a Mesh-Aligned Size Distribution — dbh_to_sizeDist
code: dbh_to_sizeDist(dbh, N_intra)

title: Convert Individual Tree Sizes to a Mesh-Aligned Size Distribution — dbh_to_sizeDist
text: dbh Numeric vector. Individual tree diameters mm. N_intra List. Size distribution object.

title: Convert Individual Tree Sizes to a Mesh-Aligned Size Distribution — dbh_to_sizeDist
text: copy N_intra $Nvec replaced count individuals mesh size class.

what: Create an ipm_env object specifying climate drivers — env_condition
title: Create an ipm_env object specifying climate drivers — env_condition
text: Create ipm_env object specifying climate drivers

title: Create an ipm_env object specifying climate drivers — env_condition
code: env_condition(MAT, MAP)

title: Create an ipm_env object specifying climate drivers — env_condition
text: MAT Numeric scalar function(t). Mean Annual Temperature Celsius. Use function time-varying climate scenarios. MAP Numeric scalar function(t). Mean Annual Precipitation mm/year.

title: Create an ipm_env object specifying climate drivers — env_condition
text: object S3 class "ipm_env" fields $MAT $MAP (unscaled; internal scaling applied engines).

title: Create an ipm_env object specifying climate drivers — env_condition
code: env <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) print(env) #> <ipm_env>  MAT=8.0°C  MAP=1200 mm/yr

what: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
text: Compute asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
code: lambda(mod, pars, stand, env, ctrl = NULL)

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
text: mod ipm_spModel object. pars ipm_parameters object. Must contain parameters least one species mod. Lambda computed species present mod pars 2014 species stand used competitors appear output. stand ipm_stand object. Provides size distributions species (focal competitors). env ipm_env object. Climate drivers. ctrl ipm_control object NULL. bin_width delta_time used. NULL, defaults used.

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
text: object S3 class "ipm_lambda" 2014 named numeric vector one element per focal species (species available parameters).

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") env  <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) ctrl <- control(years = 5, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) lam  <- lambda(mod, pars, s, env, ctrl) print(lam) #> <ipm_lambda>  draw=mean #>   ABIBAL: 2.0762

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
text: Assembles full Integral Projection Model kernel (K = P + F) focal species given size distribution vectors environmental conditions.

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
code: mkKernel(   Nvec_intra,   Nvec_inter,   delta_time,   plotSize,   Temp,   Prec,   pars,   plot_random )

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
text: Nvec_intra List. Intraspecific size distribution. Nvec_inter List. Interspecific size distribution. delta_time Numeric. Time step years. plotSize Numeric. Plot area square meters. Temp Numeric. Mean annual temperature (degrees Celsius). Prec Numeric. Mean annual precipitation (mm). pars Named list. Species-specific parameters elements growth, mort, rec, sizeIngrowth. plot_random Numeric vector length 3. Plot-level random effects growth (1), mortality (2), recruitment (3).

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
text: named list elements K (full kernel), P (growth x survival kernel), F (recruitment kernel), square matrix dimension equal number mesh points.

what: Resolve a single parameter realization from Bayesian posteriors — parameters
title: Resolve a single parameter realization from Bayesian posteriors — parameters
text: Resolve single parameter realization Bayesian posteriors

title: Resolve a single parameter realization from Bayesian posteriors — parameters
code: parameters(mod, draw = "random", seed = NULL)

title: Resolve a single parameter realization from Bayesian posteriors — parameters
text: mod ipm_spModel object. draw Character "mean" "random", positive integer 1-1000 selecting specific posterior draw index. seed Integer NULL. Random seed. draw = "random" seed = NULL, seed auto-generated draw reproducible. Retrieve $seed returned object.

title: Resolve a single parameter realization from Bayesian posteriors — parameters
text: object S3 class "ipm_parameters".

title: Resolve a single parameter realization from Bayesian posteriors — parameters
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") print(pars) #> <ipm_parameters>  draw=mean | 1 species

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
text: Plot ipm_projection object

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
code: # S3 method for class 'ipm_projection' plot(x, type = NULL, timestep = NULL, ...)

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
text: x ipm_projection object returned project. type Character NULL. One "lambda", "pop_size", "size_dist", "lambda_vs_n". NULL (default), four figures rendered sequence. timestep Integer NULL. used type = "size_dist". stored timestep (year) plot. NULL (default), last stored timestep used. Must one values x$years. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
text: x, invisibly.

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = seq(130, 600, by = 50),                  species_id = "ABIBAL", plot_size = 400) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") env  <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) ctrl <- control(years = 5, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) proj <- project(mod, pars, s, env, ctrl) plot(proj, type = "lambda")  plot(proj, type = "size_dist", timestep = 3)

what: Project population dynamics over time — project
title: Project population dynamics over time — project
text: Project population dynamics time

title: Project population dynamics over time — project
code: project(mod, pars, stand, env, ctrl)

title: Project population dynamics over time — project
text: mod ipm_spModel object. pars ipm_parameters object. stand ipm_stand object. Initial stand state (t = 0). env ipm_env object. Climate drivers (may time-varying). ctrl ipm_control object. Required.

title: Project population dynamics over time — project
text: object S3 class "ipm_projection" fields: $species, $years, $lambda, $stand_series (list ipm_dist_snapshot objects, holding continuous size distribution per species timestep), $summary.

title: Project population dynamics over time — project
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") env  <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) ctrl <- control(years = 5, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) proj <- project(mod, pars, s, env, ctrl) print(proj) #> <ipm_projection>  1 species | 5 years | store_every=1 | draw=mean

title: Scale environmental values to [0, 1] — scale_env
text: Scales mean annual temperature precipitation [0, 1] range using observed ranges stored package. scaled values IPM vital rate functions expect internally.

title: Scale environmental values to [0, 1] — scale_env
code: scale_env(MAT, MAP)

title: Scale environmental values to [0, 1] — scale_env
text: MAT Numeric. Mean annual temperature degrees Celsius. MAP Numeric. Mean annual precipitation mm/year.

title: Scale environmental values to [0, 1] — scale_env
text: named list elements MAT MAP, scaled [0, 1].

title: Scale environmental values to [0, 1] — scale_env
code: scaled <- scale_env(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200)

what: Set plot-level random effects for one or more species — set_random_effects
title: Set plot-level random effects for one or more species — set_random_effects
text: Populates $random_effects slot inside species entry ipm_parameters object. Engines (lambda(), project()) default c(0, 0, 0) species whose slot NULL.

title: Set plot-level random effects for one or more species — set_random_effects
code: set_random_effects(pars, values, species = NULL)

title: Set plot-level random effects for one or more species — set_random_effects
text: pars ipm_parameters object. values numeric vector length 3 (growth, survival, recruitment offsets), applied species named species; named list keyed species ID, element numeric vector length 3. species Character vector species IDs. Used values numeric vector; ignored values named list. NULL values numeric vector, applies species.

title: Set plot-level random effects for one or more species — set_random_effects
text: modified ipm_parameters object.

title: Set plot-level random effects for one or more species — set_random_effects
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s     <- stand(df) mod   <- species_model(s) pars  <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") pars2 <- set_random_effects(pars, values = c(0, 0, 0))

code: species_model(x, on_missing = "error")

title: Create an ipm_spModel object defining which species to model — species_model
text: x ipm_stand object character vector species IDs. on_missing Character. Behavior species ID supported_species(). One "error" (default), "drop", "static".

title: Create an ipm_spModel object defining which species to model — species_model

code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s   <- stand(df) mod <- species_model(s) print(mod) #> <ipm_spModel>  1 species: ABIBAL

what: Create an ipm_stand object representing a forest plot — stand
title: Create an ipm_stand object representing a forest plot — stand
text: Create ipm_stand object representing forest plot

title: Create an ipm_stand object representing a forest plot — stand
code: stand(data)

title: Create an ipm_stand object representing a forest plot — stand
text: data data.frame tibble individual-level tree records. Required columns: size column (named size dbh, mm,= 127 mm), species column (named sp, species, orspecies_id), plot_size column (plot area m2,value rows).

title: Create an ipm_stand object representing a forest plot — stand
text: object S3 class "ipm_stand" fields $trees (data.frame standardized columns size_mm species_id), $species (character vector unique species IDs), $plot_size (numeric scalar).

title: Create an ipm_stand object representing a forest plot — stand
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s <- stand(df) print(s) #> <ipm_stand>  3 trees | 1 species | 1000 m2 plot

what: Return the tibble of species supported by forestIPM — supported_species
title: Return the tibble of species supported by forestIPM — supported_species
text: Return tibble species supported forestIPM

title: Return the tibble of species supported by forestIPM — supported_species
code: supported_species()

title: Return the tibble of species supported by forestIPM — supported_species
text: tibble six columns: species_id, common_name, nom_commun, growth_model, surv_model, recruit_model. One row per supported species.

title: Return the tibble of species supported by forestIPM — supported_species
code: sp <- supported_species() head(sp) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   species_id species_name common_name nom_commun MAT_min MAT_max MAP_min MAP_max #>   <chr>      <chr>        <chr>       <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 ABIBAL     Abies balsa… Balsam fir  Sapin bau…    -5.2     9      433     1859 #> 2 ACERUB     Acer rubrum  Red maple   Erable ro…    -1.5    24.7    436     2688 #> 3 ACESAC     Acer saccha… Sugar maple Erable a …    -0.5    16.7    443     1944 #> 4 BETALL     Betula alle… Yellow bir… Bouleau j…    -1.4    15.1    529     2177 #> 5 BETPAP     Betula papy… White birch Bouleau b…    -5      13.5    395     1859 #> 6 CARGLA     Carya glabra Pignut hic… Caryer gl…     6.3    23.3    631.    2326 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: growth_model <chr>, surv_model <chr>, recruit_model <chr>

what: Unscale environmental values from [0, 1] to natural units — unscale_env
title: Unscale environmental values from [0, 1] to natural units — unscale_env
text: Reverses scaling applied scale_env(), converting scaled values back degrees Celsius mm/year.

title: Unscale environmental values from [0, 1] to natural units — unscale_env
code: unscale_env(MAT, MAP)

title: Unscale environmental values from [0, 1] to natural units — unscale_env
text: MAT Numeric. Scaled mean annual temperature [0, 1]. MAP Numeric. Scaled mean annual precipitation [0, 1].

title: Unscale environmental values from [0, 1] to natural units — unscale_env
text: named list elements MAT (degrees Celsius) MAP (mm/year).

title: Unscale environmental values from [0, 1] to natural units — unscale_env
code: scaled   <- scale_env(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) original <- unscale_env(scaled$MAT, scaled$MAP)
